target_graph_prediction: Feature-aligned pro-forms should support reference tracking and acceptability without making reference success sufficient for licensing.
pass_condition: Reference identification and acceptability are high for feature-aligned items while grammaticality attribution remains separable from reference success.
fail_condition: Reference success directly predicts licensing/grammar attribution with no channel separation.
notes: Baseline calibration; not the unresolved policy cell.

target_graph_prediction: Reference tracking should remain stable while policy/audience/norm-centre framing shifts acceptability attribution sanction or norm orientation.
pass_condition: Same target sentence has stable reference identification across frames but response-channel shifts in acceptability attribution sanction or norm orientation.

target_graph_prediction: True reference ambiguity should lower reference tracking across audience frames; policy framing may change sanction but should not rescue the reference path.
pass_condition: Ambiguous items produce low or split reference identification regardless of audience frame while sanction/attribution may vary.
fail_condition: Audience/policy framing alone repairs reference identification in an item with unchanged referential ambiguity.
notes: Protects against mistaking reference failure for social disagreement.

target_graph_prediction: Personhood framing should change pronoun feature fit and possibly acceptability without directly entailing grammaticality attribution.

fail_condition: Personhood ascription directly determines grammar attribution with no feature/reference/acceptability mediation.
